gloss_status: my wife gave a cry and at once ran into the depths of the grove.
column6: The Japanese links the cry and flight tightly; it does not specify what she cried.

english_gloss: The robber sprang after her at once
gloss_status: but apparently did not even catch her sleeve. I merely watched the scene as though it were an illusion.
column6: らしい marks the samurai's limited or inferential access.

gloss_status: the robber took the sword and the bow and arrows
translation_note: then cut through my rope at only one place. “Now I must look to myself.” I remember him muttering this as he disappeared from the grove.
column7: 今度はおれの身の上だ is idiomatic and context-sensitive; the gloss preserves its self-regarding turn without treating it as a formal evidence label.

gloss_status: everything was quiet. No—someone was still crying. As I untied the rope
column7: いや explicitly revises the preceding perception of complete silence.

english_gloss: But when I became aware of it
gloss_status: was that voice not my own crying?
column6: The rhetorical form marks delayed self-recognition.

english_gloss: At last I raised my exhausted body from the foot of the cedar. In front of me
gloss_status: a dagger dropped by my wife was shining.
column6: 小刀 is glossed as dagger for accessibility; the Japanese term remains canonical.

english_gloss: I took it and drove it into my chest in a single thrust. Something with the raw smell of blood rose into my mouth. Yet there was no pain at all. As my chest grew cold
column6: 腥い塊 is deliberately not normalised to blood alone; the phrase presents an embodied but indistinct mass.

gloss_status: what stillness. Not a single small bird came to sing in the sky above this grove in the mountain's shade. Only a lonely patch of sunlight drifted over the tips of the cedars and bamboo. The light—this too gradually faded. Soon I could no longer see the cedars or bamboo. I lay there
column7: The gloss preserves the progressive loss of visual and auditory access without interpreting it as a medically established moment of death.

gloss_status: someone approached me with stealthy steps. I tried to look in that direction
translation_note: but at some point a dim darkness had gathered around me.
column7: 誰か is unidentified; the segment does not establish identity

gloss_status: with a hand I could not see
translation_note: gently drew the dagger from my chest. At the same time
column6: blood once more flooded into my mouth. After that
column7: I sank forever into the darkness of chūu.
column9: 見えない modifies the hand as unavailable to sight
column10: not necessarily supernatural. 中有 is retained as chūu because it names an intermediate post-death state and should not be silently collapsed into generic darkness.
